# 5 Easy DIY Projects for Beginners

[Emma Capar](https://diyhuntress.com/author/ecapar/) [Leave a comment](https://diyhuntress.com/5-easy-diy-projects-for-beginners/#respond)

![5 Easy DIY Projects for Beginners](https://diyhuntress.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/image-1-700x394.webp)

If you’re looking to get started in the DIY world it can sometimes feel intimidating to pick a project that will both challenge you while still being achievable. So I’ve rounded up 5 projects that are great for the DIYer who’s just getting started. You’ll need some tools but not a ton, and a lot of the techniques you’ll see are super useful in the DIY space. With each project I have a corresponding YouTube video and a more detailed blog linked. So no matter what you decide to start with, you’ll have a step by step guide as you go! There’s nothing more therapeutic for me than building something, and I hope you can find that same joy in these projects too!

*This post contains affiliate links, which help support more projects on my website! For my full disclosure policy regarding affiliate links,* [*click here*](https://diyhuntress.com/policies/)*!*

## Best projects for beginners

Want to see any of these projects in action? Make sure to [subscribe to my channel](https://www.youtube.com/diyhuntress)!

Ok, here we go!

## 1. Limewash Paint an Accent Wall

Watch the full project video [here](https://youtu.be/6VRIaoQcSyM)!

Some quick tips for this project:

This project taught me a few things about limewash paint. But trust me, this is totally a great way to get into DIYing. You don’t need a ton of supplies and the visual impact at the end of the project is dramatic.

You’re going to want to make sure that you prep your wall by patching any holes and sanding the [filler](http://homedepot.sjv.io/QOB6jA) smooth.

Once you get into the actual painting process, the [primer](https://www.amazon.com/Color-Atelier-Matte-Primer-Paint/dp/B0861LT2X9?crid=7ZJPOFRSIXWQ&keywords=james%2Balexander%2Blime%2Bwash%2Bprimer&qid=1706323347&sprefix=james%2Balexander%2Blime%2Bwash%2Bprime%2Caps%2C108&sr=8-1&linkCode=sl1&tag=dihu-20&linkId=c1382f032ee4b56bab6a1a2c1105a825&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl&th=1) coats are really important and so are your brush strokes. Bigger brush strokes had a way better look in my opinion.

Keep in mind, with [limewash paint](https://www.amazon.com/Color-Atelier-Lime-Paint-Natural/dp/B07PXKY7GK?crid=1JAKX9J4NZM8S&keywords=jane%2Balexander%2Blimewash&qid=1706323298&sprefix=james%2Balexander%2Blimewash%2B%2Caps%2C83&sr=8-2-fkmr1&th=1&linkCode=sl1&tag=dihu-20&linkId=e34eb24c46094edc6aa9bc5513ccd5d1&language=en_US&ref_=as_li_ss_tl) it doesn’t look amazing at first! It needs 12 hours to dry so trust the process and you’re totally going to love it by the end.

Bonus tip: Make sure you use [painter’s tape](http://homedepot.sjv.io/yR0AA2) for your edges otherwise vertical lines made with limewash paint are super visible and will ruin the overall look.

Check out the full project in this blog [post](https://diyhuntress.com/how-to-limewash-paint-an-accent-wall/).

## 2. DIY Pegboard Wall Organizer

Watch the full project video [here](https://youtu.be/J7Zv1n6Y0XU)!

The basics for this project:

The awesome part of this project is that I started it with a scrap piece of wood. So it’s a great way to recycle leftover material. You’ll use a [circular saw](http://homedepot.sjv.io/BXraJL) to cut down your material if needed. If you don’t have a circular saw or your base piece is already the correct size you can skip that step.

You can decide what the layout of your pegs will be and you’ll use a [forstner bit](https://www.homedepot.com/p/DIABLO-3-4-in-High-Speed-Steel-Forstner-Bit-FB-005/100098840?irgwc=1&cm_mmc=afl-ir-1243126-456723-&clickid=RYpwqMW5FxyPUv-RfgSNnVasUkHTU-Us6UiNTg0) to drill the holes for the dowels.

As with any good woodworking project you’ll get some sanding practice in and you can customize it by adding shelves if you want but they’re totally optional. This is such a fun one day project that will get you using a few power tools!

You’ll find more detailed instructions [here](https://diyhuntress.com/diy-pegboard-wall-organizer/)!

## 3. DIY Hexagon Serving Tray

Watch the full project video [here](https://youtu.be/FUvtyLSj2Jo)!

The rundown of this project:

I will say there is some math involved with getting the correct measurements for hexagons. But I also made a [template](https://diyhuntress.com/wp-content/uploads/2019/04/DIY-Huntress-Hexagon-Tray-Template.pdf) for the same size as the tray I made, so feel free to use mine if that takes away the guess work for you!

You can create your own geometric design in the center of your tray and once you finalize your design you’ll make angled cuts on the [miter saw](https://amzn.to/3vLplTH).

You’ll sand and [stain](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B01ELF6TWW/ref=as_li_qf_asin_il_tl?ie=UTF8&tag=dihu-20&creative=9325&linkCode=as2&creativeASIN=B01ELF6TWW&linkId=10f55ea2c89aaf2a8f8b992e27ebbd71) your pieces to get your desired look. Attach the pieces with [glue](https://amzn.to/3ANecEQ) and a [pin nailer](http://homedepot.sjv.io/zaEG3M). You’ll use this same method to attach the edges of the tray.

Once you assemble and finish [staining](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000ONVIC2/ref=as_li_qf_asin_il_tl?ie=UTF8&tag=dihu-20&creative=9325&linkCode=as2&creativeASIN=B000ONVIC2&linkId=2041209a9bdb630e100c1b475cf836c9) you’ll have a perfect serving tray that you made yourself!

Take a look at the full blog [post](https://diyhuntress.com/diy-hexagon-serving-tray/) for more details.

## 4. DIY Easy Smokeless Fire Pit Set Up

Watch the full project video [here](https://youtu.be/JYsv9HzDC_I)!

What you really need to know about this project:

This project was such a satisfying and quick win. I was able to make a super functional and aesthetically pleasing outdoor space out of a spot that was just nothing in my backyard. You’ll need minimal tools for this project and the impact is dramatic.

I used [cut to size pavers](https://www.lowes.com/pd/Riccobene-Terra-Shell-Articulating-Paving-Mat-Slate-Light-Gray-Concrete-Patio-Stone-Common-12-25-in-x-23-5-in-Actual-12-25-in-x-23-5-in/1001857612?cm_mmc=shp-_-c-_-prd-_-lwn-_-ggl-_-LIA_LWN_236_Hardscapes-_-1001857612-_-local-_-0-_-0&gad_source=1&gclid=Cj0KCQjwhtWvBhD9ARIsAOP0Gojz9UMW7bjQ17GK09DtoR7DXtXdL8Xaf3HnZD_IIIoaXOP0yzLu060aAoyNEALw_wcB&gclsrc=aw.ds) to create a spot for my smokeless fire pit. Your creativity itch will be satisfied as you design an organic shape to lay them in.

After you get your fire pit base set up it’s all styling. I got some some chairs, pillows, and mosquito repellant twinkle lights to create the perfect s’mores station. You can really make this fit the style of your home however you’d like.

This one is such a fun and quick weekend project that you can start in the afternoon and be enjoying s’mores by the evening.

There are more detailed instructions [here](https://diyhuntress.com/diy-easy-smokeless-fire-pit-set-up/)!

## 5. DIY Record Cabinet Restoration

Watch the full project video [here](https://youtu.be/sGrb3JrsW8w).

The nitty gritty:

This same restoration process could totally be applied to any type of older furniture. So if the size of this cabinet seems daunting you can definitely start with a smaller piece.

Restoring furniture takes a lot of cleaning, sanding, wood filling, more sanding and finishing (by staining or sealing the wood). Some pieces also have lots of nooks and crannies so you sometimes have to get creative with how you sand in those small spaces. Also, depending on the style of your piece you may need to keep certain parts in their original condition, like with this cabinet I wanted to keep the fabric of the speaker and the record mechanisms as is.

Make sure to notice if your piece is solid wood or plywood with a veneer. If you sand down a veneer too far you’ll have trouble making it look good when you get to the finishing stage. So make sure to check this before digging in with a powerful tool (don’t ask me how I know this…)

This project will really take you through the process of sanding and raising the grain of your wood so it’s a great way to learn this technique for future furniture/woodworking projects.

You can find some more step by step instructions in this [blog post](https://diyhuntress.com/diy-record-cabinet-restoration/).

## Happy DIYing

I hope you find a project on this list that inspires you to go out and make something! Remember you can make any of these projects completely your own or follow along how I did them step by step. But feel free to stretch your creativity and make something that really reflects you.

Also, if you’re really new to the DIY space make sure to check out my blog post [5 Best Power Tools for Beginner Woodworkers and DIYers](https://diyhuntress.com/5-best-power-tools-for-beginner-woodworkers-and-diyers/). That post has recommendations for where and how to start your tool collection and I hope it helps you make some projects with confidence! As always you can find me on [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/@diyhuntress/videos) where I share all of my current projects. But in the meantime, happy DIYing friends!

## **Save This for Later**

I hope you enjoyed this project! If so, make sure to [Pin It](https://www.pinterest.com/diyhuntress/) for later!

Title: 15 DIY Projects That Can Be Done In A Day Or Weekend
[Skip to content](#main)


[![DIY Playbook](https://thediyplaybook.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/08/primary-logo.png)](https://thediyplaybook.com/)

[![DIY Playbook](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='1200'%20height='652'%20viewBox='0%200%201200%20652'%3E%3C/svg%3E)](https://thediyplaybook.com/)



[Home](https://thediyplaybook.com/) » [DIY Index](https://thediyplaybook.com/diy-index/) » [DIY](https://thediyplaybook.com/category/diy/) » [Do It In A Day](https://thediyplaybook.com/category/diy/do-it-in-a-day/)

# 15 DIY Projects That Can Be Done in a Day or Weekend

By[Casey Finn](https://thediyplaybook.com/about)  Posted on

When I was [re-doing the website](https://thediyplaybook.com/2022-new-diy-playbook-website/) in the spring, I spent time reviewing all of the projects I’ve shared here on the blog, and I tried my best to figure out categories that would make the most sense for my audience. After noticing lots of simple home projects that can be done fairly quickly, I came up with the category of [“Do It In A Day”](https://thediyplaybook.com/category/diy/do-it-in-a-day/).

I went through many old posts and added them to this newfound category. You can see them all here. Each of these DIY home projects is a great way to make a big impact and bring a fresh look to your space, without too much time.

You’ll find lots of great starter project options and others that require some power tools. Regardless, you can tackle any of these projects with my step-by-step guide – I believe in you!

[![Finding time for DIY projects in Ask Casey No. 14](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='1024'%20height='683'%20viewBox='0%200%201024%20683'%3E%3C/svg%3E)](https://thediyplaybook.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/nursery-picture-frame-molding-40-1-scaled.jpg)

[Picture Frame Molding](https://thediyplaybook.com/picture-frame-molding/)

Today, I thought it would be fun to take a trip down memory lane and share some of my favorite, simple home projects. I know lack of time is a hurdle when it comes to doing projects around the house. And now, as a busy mom of two, I understand that more than ever!

But there are so many things you can do to your home that won’t take a weekend or month to complete. And if you’re still struggling to make time for home projects, [this post has lots of great tips](https://thediyplaybook.com/make-time-diy/)!

## Time-Saving Tip

In This Post

[Toggle](#)

If you only have time to tackle projects on the weekend, I suggest doing all your prep work during the week. That means getting familiar with what goes into the project, buying all of the supplies at the hardware store (trust me, it will be way less crowded!), and prepping the space. That way, you’re all ready to dive in on Saturday morning!

## 15 Simple Home Projects

Sometimes I think the best part of doing home DIY projects is that they give me such a feeling of accomplishment and pride. Whether it’s [a huge DIY built-in project](https://thediyplaybook.com/office-built-ins-reveal/) or [a small organized drawer](https://thediyplaybook.com/organized-makeup-drawer/), I get that same amazing feeling! And I want you to feel it too. So let’s take a look at this list and maybe something will spark your interest to try in your own space.

## Make a Bathtub Tray

We’re taking it super old-school with [this DIY project](https://thediyplaybook.com/easy-diy-bathtub-tray/) that I completed in our old condo. I’m a big bath person and I love taking baths, especially in the winter. Instead of shelling out big bucks for a custom bath tray, I made my own with some scrap wood. [Here’s the full tutorial](https://thediyplaybook.com/easy-diy-bathtub-tray/).

**Estimated Time:** 1 hour

## DIY Abstract Art & Canvas

Last year, I took an old canvas that my mom was donating and created [a special piece of art for Ellis’ nursery](https://thediyplaybook.com/diy-abstract-art/). I have zero experience when it comes to painting, so abstract was the way to go! If you can let go of your perfectionist instincts (easier said than done, I know!), then this is an easy project and one that can really be so much fun! Once it was dry, [I built a simple frame](https://thediyplaybook.com/diy-canvas-frame/) to give it a high-end look.

**Estimated Time:** A full day if you’re tackling both the painting and the frame.

## Use Rub ‘N Buff

Oh my goodness, [this little tube](https://amzn.to/3bRvwPC) is pure magic. I first used this when I wanted to take my bedroom curtain rods from black to gold. The name says it all – you rub it in and buff the color. Boom, your item is instantly gold.

If you’re using it on a relatively small item, it’s a less messy alternative to spray paint, too. I often get asked if the curtain rods have held up. They still look perfect, even with daily use! Have something in your house that you want to make gold? Give this little product a try with [my tutorial](https://thediyplaybook.com/rub-n-buff-curtain-rod/).

**Estimated Time:** 30 minutes

## Paint Your Vanity

Since moving into this home, I’ve painted three vanities in three different ways. After trying out different methods and techniques, I recommend [this step-by-step tutorial](https://thediyplaybook.com/cabinet-transformations-kit/) because it takes the least amount of time and the finish came out the best. You use a cabinet paint kit and there’s no sanding or priming necessary! It’s truly amazing to see what a fresh coat of paint does to revive a space and bring a more modern look.

**Estimated Time:** One full day

## Hang a Gallery Wall

I love a good gallery wall and I have multiples throughout our home. My favorite is [Rory’s gallery wall](https://thediyplaybook.com/nursery-gallery-wall/) because it has so many special pieces. Gather your favorite art, photos, and mementos, and have some fun creating a beautiful arrangement. [This blog post](https://thediyplaybook.com/rookie-mistake-hanging-wall-art/) outlines my best tips when it comes to the actual hanging part!

**Estimated Time:** 30 minutes

## Paint Your Outdated Tile

[![My best tips to paint floor tile](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='1024'%20height='1536'%20viewBox='0%200%201024%201536'%3E%3C/svg%3E)](https://thediyplaybook.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/09/basement-bathroom-floor-tile-63-scaled.jpg)

[Our Basement Bathroom Floor Tile](https://thediyplaybook.com/tips-to-paint-floor-tile/)

Here’s another project that I tackled not one, but three times! Ha! I painted three of our bathroom floors and stenciled them all. This was a relatively simple way to bring new life to the old tile, for a fraction of the cost of new flooring.

Can the full stencil design be done in one day? No, that’s more of a weekend project because of dry time. But, if you want to just [paint your tile one color](https://thediyplaybook.com/how-to-paint-tile-floors/), then that can be done in a day. If you’re looking to take it to the next level, you’ll need one more day of work!

**Estimated Time:** One full day

## Make a Dry Erase Board

I adore [the acrylic dry-erase board](https://thediyplaybook.com/diy-acrylic-dry-erase-board/) I made for our office. However, I will admit that I don’t use it nearly enough! But it sure is pretty. I like the idea of using something like this for [a family command center](https://thediyplaybook.com/finn-family-meetings/).

**Estimated Time:** 2 hours

## Organize Something!

[![How to create drawers for a kid-friendly kitchen](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='1024'%20height='683'%20viewBox='0%200%201024%20683'%3E%3C/svg%3E)](https://thediyplaybook.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/01/organized-kitchen-drawers-6-scaled.jpg)

[Organizing Our Kids’ Food Supplies](https://thediyplaybook.com/kid-friendly-kitchen/)

I’ve done countless organizational projects around our house, from [my small makeup drawer](https://thediyplaybook.com/organized-makeup-drawer/) to [our entire upstairs](https://thediyplaybook.com/closet-organization-tips/)! Each project leaves me feeling happy and motivated. I encourage you to find a space that’s been bugging you and give it a good refresh – especially THAT storage space that becomes your “catch all”. This post has some good tips and my fave organizing supplies.

**Estimated Time:** From 10 minutes to a full day

## Add Some Wood To Your Walls

I think the best way to make a home look more high-end is with a wood wall treatment. I’ve done so many of these projects over the years – from [chair rail in Rory’s room](https://thediyplaybook.com/how-to-add-chair-rail/), to [picture frame molding in Ellis’ room](https://thediyplaybook.com/picture-frame-molding/), to [molding in our bathroom](https://thediyplaybook.com/video-wall-molding/).

The time it takes will depend on the difficulty of the project and the space you’re tackling, but small spaces can be done in a day! We were able to add the molding to our bathroom in one day and it’s my favorite feature in that space. A woodworking project is a sure way to bring an elevated design style to a blank wall or stairwell ([a project that I am SO proud of!](https://thediyplaybook.com/stairwell-accent-wall/))

**Estimated Time:** One full day

## Install a Light Fixture or Ceiling Fan

Swapping out a dated, existing light fixture or ceiling fan, is a simple home project. Trust me, you don’t have to be an electrician to get the job done. New lighting will instantly update your space!

[This blog post](https://thediyplaybook.com/how-to-install-a-light-fixture/) walks you through the exact steps to install a new light fixture, while [this one](https://thediyplaybook.com/new-ceiling-fan-tips-install/) has the ceiling fan tutorial. Oh, and while you’re at it, go ahead and [swap your light switch for a dimmer switch](https://www.instagram.com/p/CgAKVvFjyTC/)!

**Estimated Time:** 2 hours

## Paint Your Front Door or A Full Room

If your home’s curb appeal could use a boost, I highly recommend [painting your front door](https://thediyplaybook.com/fall-front-porch/). Ours is the color “Blue Note” by Benjamin Moore and I even [painted the door to our garage the same color](https://thediyplaybook.com/paint-metal-door/)! [This blog post](https://thediyplaybook.com/5-tips-for-painting-a-front-door/) has all of my best tips for painting a door.

As for your home’s interior, the easiest and most budget-friendly way to update a room is through paint. It’s a beginner DIY project and a fun one at that! I break down all of my do’s and don’ts when it comes to painting in [this blog post](https://thediyplaybook.com/rookie-mistakes-painting/) as well as the [paint colors we used in our house in this post](https://thediyplaybook.com/warm-paint-color-palette/).

**Estimated Time:** 2 hours to a full day

## Hang Wallpaper

[![How to wallpaper a closet](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='1024'%20height='1536'%20viewBox='0%200%201024%201536'%3E%3C/svg%3E)](https://thediyplaybook.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/07/wallpaper-nursery-closet-32-scaled.jpg)

[Wallpapering Ellis’ Closet](https://thediyplaybook.com/wallpaper-a-nursery-closet/)

Ohhh, wallpaper, I used to be so afraid of it! Now that I’ve installed it in our home four times (!!!), I actually look forward to using it in a space. It can add so much pattern, life, and excitement to a room.

[![How to wallpaper a ceiling](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20width='1024'%20height='1536'%20viewBox='0%200%201024%201536'%3E%3C/svg%3E)](https://thediyplaybook.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/02/wallpaper-bedroom-ceiling-32-scaled.jpg)

[Wallpapering a Ceiling](https://thediyplaybook.com/wallpaper-the-ceiling/)

Just take a look at [our bedroom ceiling](https://thediyplaybook.com/wallpaper-the-ceiling/)! (Although, I probably wouldn’t start with a ceiling for your first wallpaper project. Ha!) And if you’re afraid to use the “real” stuff, [this blog post](https://thediyplaybook.com/install-peel-and-stick-wallpaper/) walks you through the steps to install peel-and-stick wallpaper. Peel-and-stick wallpaper is also an easy way to bring some personality to your living spaces if you’re renting, too!

**Estimated Time:** Half day

## Hang Window Treatments

A lot of the design questions I get revolve around choosing window treatments. And that makes sense because every home has windows and everyone needs privacy! I walk through my best tips to choose window treatments in [this blog post](https://thediyplaybook.com/how-to-choose-window-treatments/).

As for installation tutorials, here’s [how to hang curtains](https://thediyplaybook.com/blackout-nursery-curtains/), [how to install solar shades](https://thediyplaybook.com/video-how-to-install-solar-shades/), and [how to install bamboo shades](https://thediyplaybook.com/blackout-bamboo-shades-video/). I’ve got you covered!

**Estimated Time:** 2-3 hours

## Hang Something From The Ceiling

This is a recent project that I tackled in my home office. I wanted to add some more greenery and found the perfect brass planter (unfortunately it’s no longer available; [but this one looks stunning too!](https://rstyle.me/+Dhww0a_zbbvtNc8WJQO3RQ)). If you’re looking to hang a planter or anything else from the ceiling, [this blog post](https://thediyplaybook.com/hang-items-from-the-ceiling/) walks you through the exact steps for a secure hold.

**Estimated Time:** 20 minutes

## Seal Your Marble or Porcelain

Don’t forget about home maintenance. These are very important, simple home projects! If you have marble anywhere in your home, I recommend following [the tips from this blog post](https://thediyplaybook.com/seal-marble-dining-room-table/) to seal it. If you have porcelain tile, [this blog post](https://thediyplaybook.com/how-to-clean-and-seal-porcelain-tile-and-grout/) will walk you through the best tips to clean and seal your tile and grout.

**Estimated Time:** 5 minutes to an hour

Ready for more projects? [This blog post dives into 80+ DIY home improvement projects you can tackle this weekend!](https://thediyplaybook.com/home-improvement-ideas/)

Casey

# 10 DIY Home Projects for Beginners

Looking to refresh your space without sinking a lot of money or time? You’re in the right place! I’m all about those DIY home projects that make a big impact without taking up your entire weekend. With 10 DIY home projects for beginners, you are sure to find a project you can tackle, even without experience. Whether it’s sprucing up the living room, giving your front door a new life, or crafting something unique with a little paint, these simple projects are a great way to breathe new energy into your home.

Grab your basic tools, a tape measure, and maybe some coffee, because we’re diving into 10 projects that are beginner-friendly, fun, and super satisfying to complete in a day! They may be simple but they will improve the look of your space and make you feel better about what you can accomplish.

![Modern Living room, white couch with a big window and lighting.](https://thunderbunnyco.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/Modern-Living-room--1024x576.png)

## **1. DIY Home Projects for Beginners: A Great Way to Start**

Starting small is the *best way* to build confidence in your *DIY journey*. These quick and impactful projects prove that you don’t need *power tools* or a *lot of time* to make a difference.

### **Upgrade Light Fixtures**

Changing your *light fixtures* can instantly transform the *living space*. Swap out old ones for modern, stylish options that match your vibe.

* In the *living room*, try a bold chandelier or a sleek pendant light to create a stunning *focal point*.
* In the kitchen, add under-cabinet lights for functional and ambient lighting.
* For bedrooms, replace table lamps with wall-mounted sconces to free up nightstand space.

Related post: [Bathroom Light LED Fixtures : A Bright Idea For Your Home](https://thunderbunnyco.com/bathroom-light-led-fixtures-a-bright-idea-for-your-home/)

**Pro Tip:** Don’t skip dimmer switches! They’re an *easy project* that gives you control over the lighting mood.

### **Refresh Your Front Door**

Your *front door* sets the tone for your home. A *fresh coat of paint* is all it takes to make a statement.

* **Colors to Try:**
  + **Red:** Bold and classic.
  + **Navy Blue:** Sophisticated and timeless.
  + **Sage Green:** Warm and inviting.

Pair the new paint with updated house numbers, a fresh doormat, and maybe even a shiny new knocker for a polished look. This quick project will give your home instant curb appeal, and it’s the perfect DIY project for a beginner.

![mosaic tile backsplash](https://thunderbunnyco.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/Mosaic-Tile-Backsplash-1024x576.png)

### **Add a New Backsplash**

A backsplash is a quick *DIY home improvement project* that packs a punch.

* **Peel-and-Stick Tiles:** Perfect for renters or *beginner DIY* enthusiasts.
* **Shiplap:** Adds rustic charm to kitchens or bathrooms.
* **Chalkboard Paint:** Functional and fun, especially for family spaces.

### **Reorganize a Closet**

Closets often get overlooked, but a little organization can change that.

* Add hooks for scarves, hats, and bags.
* Install [stick-on LED lights](https://amzn.to/3DzDrih) for better visibility.
* Use baskets or bins to tidy up loose items.

Trust me, an organized closet is a *fresh look* that makes mornings so much smoother. The last time I did a closet refresh, I would walk in just to enjoy the view.

![](https://thunderbunnyco.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/organized-closet-1024x576.png)

## **2. Easy DIY Projects for Beginners**

Let’s talk about *easy DIY projects*. These don’t require a steep *learning curve* or expensive *new tools*. They’re the perfect *great starter project* for anyone dipping their toes into the DIY world.

### **Ideas for Beginners**

* **Swap Hardware:** Replace old knobs on cabinets or furniture with brass, matte black, or even vintage crystal.
* **Decorate with Washi Tape:** Add color and patterns to picture frames, shelves, or even light switches.
* **[Install Peel-and-Stick Floor Tiles](https://amzn.to/4iN24rT):** Refresh a bathroom or laundry room floor with minimal effort.

Each of these projects adds a *whole new look* to your home without breaking the bank.

## **3. Simple Projects to Tackle in a Day**

When you only have one day to spare, these *simple home projects* are your go-to. They deliver results you’ll notice every time you walk into the room.

### **Quick Wins**

* **Update Curtains or Blinds:** Swap old window treatments for something modern, like sheer panels or blackout curtains.
* **Hang a Large Mirror:** Mirrors reflect light and make any space feel bigger. Hang one in the *living room* or *guest room* for an instant upgrade.
* **Paint an Accent Wall:** Choose a bold color or *stick wallpaper* to create a striking feature in your room.

![](https://thunderbunnyco.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/handmade-wooden-storage-bench-1024x576.png)

## **4. Woodworking Projects for Beginners**

Don’t let the word “woodworking” scare you off. These *beginner-friendly DIY projects* are straightforward and don’t require fancy *power tools*.

### **Ideas to Try**

* **Build a Storage Bench:** Perfect for an entryway or mudroom, and you can customize it with paint or stain.
* **Craft a Kids’ Toy Box:** Functional and charming, this project also teaches you basic joinery skills.
* **Create Wooden Picture Ledges:** Display books, photos, or art in a clean, minimalist way.

With a *nail gun* and a *circular saw*, you’ll feel like a pro in no time.

## **5. Accent Wall Living Room Ideas**

Nothing changes a room faster than an *accent wall*. Whether you’re going for bold and dramatic or soft and textured, this project is a *great way* to refresh your space.

### **Options to Explore**

* **Geometric Paint Designs:** Use *painter’s tape* to create shapes and patterns for a modern vibe.
* **Brick Veneer:** Add industrial charm to your *living space*.
* **Floating Shelves:** Turn your accent wall into a display for plants, books, or decor.

This project is ideal for renters too—*stick wallpaper* or peel-and-stick panels are easily removable.

![living room accent wall](https://thunderbunnyco.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/living-room-accent-wall-1024x576.png)

## **6. Add a Fresh Coat of Paint for a New Look**

Nothing beats a *fresh coat of paint* for instant transformation. Whether it’s walls, furniture, or even ceilings, paint is a *diy beginner’s* best friend.

### **Painting Ideas**

* **Cabinet Refresh:** Sand down your kitchen or bathroom cabinets and give them a new color.
* **Color-Block a Wall:** Use two complementary colors to divide your wall horizontally or vertically.
* **Paint the Ceiling:** Add subtle drama with a soft pastel or bold navy.

**Pro Tip:** Always use *painter’s tape* for clean edges and a polished finish.

## **7. Functional Projects That Add Value**

DIY isn’t just about making things pretty—it’s also about making your home work better for you.

### **Value-Adding Projects**

* **Install Motion-Activated Lights:** Great for closets, hallways, or outdoor areas.
* **Upgrade Smoke Detectors:** Modern options sync with your phone for peace of mind.
* **Add Hooks in the Entryway:** Simple, effective, and perfect for organizing keys, bags, and coats.

These *simple ways* to improve functionality can also appeal to *potential buyers*.

## **8. Outdoor Projects for a Quick Home Refresh**

Your outdoor space deserves as much attention as your interiors. These projects are perfect for making your porch, patio, or garden more inviting.

### **Outdoor DIY Ideas**

* **DIY Fire Pit:** Gather bricks or stones to create a cozy focal point for evenings outside.
* **Paint Outdoor Furniture:** Use weather-resistant paint to refresh chairs or tables.
* **Install Solar Path Lights:** A *great way* to illuminate your walkway while being energy-efficient.

## **9. Spruce Up Small Spaces**

Even tiny areas can have a *big impact* with the right upgrades.

### **Ideas for Small Spaces**

* **Add Vertical Storage:** Floating shelves or pegboards maximize space without crowding.
* **Upgrade Light Switch Covers:** Small changes like decorative covers can elevate a room’s look.
* **Hang Curtains High:** Mount curtain rods closer to the ceiling to make windows look larger.

These *small projects* prove that even the tiniest spaces deserve love.

![](https://thunderbunnyco.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/backyard-with-nice-patio-furniture-and-fire-pit-1024x576.png)

## **10. Makeover a Piece of Furniture**

Furniture flips are endlessly fun and so rewarding. You can breathe new life into an old *piece of furniture* with just a little effort.

### **Furniture Makeover Ideas**

* **Stencil Designs:** Add patterns to a plain coffee table or dresser for a pop of personality.
* **Two-Tone Paint Jobs:** Paint the body one color and stain the top for contrast.
* **Change the Legs:** Swapping out furniture legs can give old pieces a modern update.

This is the ultimate project for creative expression and crafting something uniquely yours.

## **Your DIY Journey Starts Here**

Every project you take on brings your home closer to the space you’ve always imagined. From *simple home projects* like a painted *front door* to more creative tasks like a *gallery wall*, DIY is all about taking small steps with *big impact*.

So grab your *basic tools*, swing by the *local hardware store*, and get started. Your home is ready for its glow-up!
